danger_signals.meaning: escalated danger state requiring tactical repositioning

danger_signals.visual_form: organized hostile combatants distributed across stage environments
danger_signals.meaning: persistent environmental threat necessitating continuous offensive response

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: third-person perspective behind helicopter; enemies render as tactical threats in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies pursue standard patrol/attack patterns; player moves through rail-based forward progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: camera shifts from third-person to top-down perspective; boss entity becomes focal point
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: escalated enemy behavior; player must adapt to new viewpoint paradigm for targeting and evasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: progression to next stage with environmental transition; new threat hierarchy established
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: new enemy formations and boss encounters replace prior threats

environmental_storytelling.example: four distinct stages with different geographic/military contexts communicate campaign scope and diverse operational theaters
increasing numbers and complexity of military vehicles across stages suggest intensifying conflict severity
camera change to top-down during boss fights signals shift from tactical movement to focused confrontation—communicates strategic importance of boss entities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to directional input; maintains rail-based forward momentum; weapons fire on player command
velocity reduction allows tactical repositioning; distinctive mechanical feature differentiates helicopter capability from generic flight
render within environment prior to player proximity; suggest organized ground operations
triggers perspective shift; occupies focal screen position; requires sustained combat engagement to defeat

notes: Super Thunder Blade communicates world-state primarily through mechanical perspective shifts and enemy density/type variation. The helicopter platform itself serves as a faction identifier—its design and armament (guns/missiles) signal military authority and combat capability. Danger is communicated through enemy presence and concentration rather than environmental hazard visualization. The political situation (military operation against guerrillas) is implicit in game structure rather than explicitly visualized through environmental markers like flags, insignia, or territorial control zones. The rail-shooter paradigm constrains manifestation complexity; world-state exists primarily as enemy configuration and camera perspective rather than dynamic environmental transformation.
